full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic airframe coupler bulkhead for a two-stage-recovery student sounding rocket flying to a declared apogee in the 4,000 to 6,000 ft range. The bulkhead is a disc with integral shoulder coupler tube that joins two body-tube sections (outer diameter 152.4 mm / 6 in, inner diameter 149.4 mm / 5.88 in) and anchors the main parachute harness via a single forged U-bolt through the disc center. Disc thickness to be sized; integral coupler shoulder length 152.4 mm (6 in); coupler outer diameter 149.4 mm to slide inside the body tube; central U-bolt hole 12.7 mm (1/2 in) on centerline; 8x M4 radial screw holes on a 144 mm PCD for shear-pin fastening to the body tube. Material: 6061-T6 aluminum (E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3). Load cases derived from two-section recovery with upper section mass 4.5 kg and lower (booster) section mass 7.5 kg, mission peak altitude 6,000 ft. (R1) Parachute-opening shock at main deployment: shock load applied at the U-bolt in the axial direction equal to 4 x (upper section weight x load factor) with an opening-shock load factor of 8 g on the upper section, giving 4.5 kg x 8 g x 4 = 1413 N axial at the U-bolt; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R2) Descent constraint: main canopy shall open such that the section descends within <=90 s total descent time; landing kinetic energy per independent section <= 75 ft-lbf (101.7 J); the bulkhead must survive the resulting landing-impact load of upper section mass times descent velocity (computed from 75 ft-lbf limit: v_max = sqrt(2*101.7/4.5) = 6.72 m/s); a 1.5x dynamic amplification factor applied as a 30 g equivalent static landing load at the U-bolt = 4.5 kg x 30 g = 1324 N; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R3) Shear-pin interface: the 8 M4 shear pins shall fail in shear at separation event (nominal shear force 500 N per pin at event) without yielding the bulkhead around the holes; local bearing stress <= yield / 1.25 = 221 MPa. (R4) Mass of the bulkhead <= 300 g. (R5) Redundant recovery: bulkhead shall accept a secondary attachment point (second U-bolt hole offset 30 mm from center) and carry LC1 entirely through the secondary path with the same stress limit <= 184 MPa. Primary verification structural FEA (linear static, 4 independent load cases). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 monolithic part, a report listing mass in g, max von Mises per load case in MPa, and redundant-load-path check.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic coupler bulkhead for a two-stage-recovery student sounding rocket (4,000-6,000 ft apogee class). The bulkhead is a 6061-T6 disc with integral coupler shoulder that joins two 6 in (152.4 mm OD / 149.4 mm ID) body-tube sections, anchors the main parachute harness through a central U-bolt, accepts 8 radial M4 shear pins on a 144 mm PCD, and includes a secondary (redundant) U-bolt hole offset 30 mm from center. Upper section mass 4.5 kg, lower (booster) section 7.5 kg.

Envelope: disc OD 149.4 mm (sliding fit), integral coupler shoulder length 152.4 mm, central 12.7 mm U-bolt hole.

Load cases.
- LC1 main-parachute opening shock: 1413 N axial tension at the primary U-bolt (upper-section mass x 8 g opening shock x 4).
- LC2 landing impact: 1324 N (30 g equivalent) at the U-bolt, landing kinetic energy capped at 75 ft-lbf (101.7 J) per section, descent time <= 90 s.
- LC3 shear-pin interface: 500 N per pin on the eight M4 holes at separation, no yielding around the hole.
- LC4 redundant path: LC1 reacted entirely through the secondary U-bolt hole.

prompt.title: Sounding rocket airframe coupler bulkhead
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic coupler bulkhead for 6 in (152 mm) airframe; sizes U-bolt, shear pins, and redundant attachment; passes parachute-opening shock and 75 ft-lbf landing KE.

boundary_conditions.mounting: coupler shoulder outer surface bonded to body-tube inner wall
boundary_conditions.loads: axial tension at U-bolt; radial bearing on pin holes

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Pressure + recovery loads are the gate; ascent dynamics not required for bulkhead.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Bulkhead is a disc in tension dominated loading.

eval_coverage: multi-scenario static (opening shock + landing + pin shear + redundant)
